title: Adenoid symptom documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.
examination.rationale: The exact paediatric OME recognition section directly reviews mouth breathing, snoring, nasal obstruction, adenoid history, hearing, communication, balance, functional impact, ENT examination, hearing testing, and tympanometry; it supplies partial evidence only and does not diagnose adenoid hypertrophy.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

investigations.text: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.
investigations.rationale: The exact reassessment section supports clinician-entered review, arranged follow-up, and questions without recommending adenoid investigation or surgery.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations

source_limitations: Age, nasal obstruction, mouth breathing, snoring, apnoea, sleep impact, recurrent infection, hearing, speech, allergy, examination, adenoid size, diagnosis, tests, referral, and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
